---
title: "Off-Target Population Selectivity Comparison"
---

# Summary
Compares selectivity scan results computed against two distinct off-target cell populations.
For a chosen target cell type, each receptor pair is plotted as a point with x = selectivity
against population 1 and y = selectivity against population 2. Points are categorized and
labeled to reveal: pairs uniquely effective against one population, and pairs that simultaneously
discriminate from both off-target populations.

# Inputs
- Two CSV files produced by `run_selectivity_scan`, one per off-target population definition
- `SCAN_PATH_1`, `SCAN_PATH_2`: paths to those CSVs
- `TARG_CELL_TYPE`: target cell type to extract from both scans
- `POP_1_LABEL`, `POP_2_LABEL`: human-readable names for each off-target population

# Outputs
- Scatter plot: x = selectivity vs. pop 1, y = selectivity vs. pop 2
  - Gray: neither population is well-discriminated
  - Blue: specific to population 1 (high x, low y)
  - Orange: specific to population 2 (high y, low x)
  - Red: effective against both populations simultaneously; point size scales with
    Euclidean distance from the origin (computed on z-score-normalized axes), and
    a reference arc marks the labeling cutoff
  - Gold stars (★): global top-N pairs by individual-axis selectivity
  - Purple diamonds (◆): user-specified pairs of interest, always labeled

```{python}
%config InlineBackend.figure_formats = ['svg']
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patheffects as pe
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

from figures.scanning_analyses.scan_runners import load_selec_scan_results

# ── Parameters ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SCAN_PATH_1 = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260212_full_geom_sweep.csv"
SCAN_PATH_2 = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260319_CD4-geom-sweep+Treg__flattened.csv"
# SCAN_PATH_2 = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260212_CD4TEM-against-all-CD4_geom.csv"

POP_1_LABEL = "All immune cells"
POP_2_LABEL = "CD4+ immune cells"

TARG_CELL_TYPE = "CD4 TEM"

# Percentile threshold (0–100) above which a receptor pair is considered "high"
# on a given axis. E.g. 95 means the top 5 % of non-NaN pairs.
HIGH_PERCENTILE = 80

# Number of auto-labeled points per category (pop1_specific, pop2_specific, both)
N_LABEL = 5

# Number of globally top-ranked pairs to highlight with a star per axis
N_TOP_OVERALL = 1

# User-specified receptor pairs to always label (names must match scan data exactly).
# Each entry is a (rec1, rec2) tuple; order does not matter.
HIGHLIGHT_PAIRS: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
    ("CD26-2", "CD26-2"),
    ("Integrin-7", "CD4-1"),
    ("CD4-1", "CD45RO"),
]

# ── Load scans ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
opt_selec_1, _, _, receptors_1, cell_types_1 = load_selec_scan_results(SCAN_PATH_1)
opt_selec_2, _, _, receptors_2, cell_types_2 = load_selec_scan_results(SCAN_PATH_2)

assert TARG_CELL_TYPE in cell_types_1, f"{TARG_CELL_TYPE} not in scan 1 cell types"
assert TARG_CELL_TYPE in cell_types_2, f"{TARG_CELL_TYPE} not in scan 2 cell types"

# ── Align receptor sets ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Use the intersection of receptors present in both scans.
common_receptors = sorted(set(receptors_1) & set(receptors_2))
assert len(common_receptors) > 0, "No common receptors between the two scans"

idx1 = {r: i for i, r in enumerate(receptors_1)}
idx2 = {r: i for i, r in enumerate(receptors_2)}
ct_idx1 = cell_types_1.index(TARG_CELL_TYPE)
ct_idx2 = cell_types_2.index(TARG_CELL_TYPE)

# ── Extract aligned selectivity vectors ───────────────────────────────────────
# Only lower-triangle pairs (including diagonal) are populated by scan_selectivity.
records = []
for ri, rec1 in enumerate(common_receptors):
    for rj, rec2 in enumerate(common_receptors):
        if ri < rj:          # upper triangle — not computed
            continue
        s1 = opt_selec_1[idx1[rec1], idx1[rec2], ct_idx1]
        s2 = opt_selec_2[idx2[rec1], idx2[rec2], ct_idx2]
        if np.isnan(s1) or np.isnan(s2):
            continue
        records.append({"rec1": rec1, "rec2": rec2, "s1": s1, "s2": s2})

df = pd.DataFrame(records)
assert len(df) > 0, "No valid (non-NaN) receptor pairs found in both scans"

# ── Categorise points ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
thresh1 = np.nanpercentile(df["s1"], HIGH_PERCENTILE)
thresh2 = np.nanpercentile(df["s2"], HIGH_PERCENTILE)

high1 = df["s1"] >= thresh1
high2 = df["s2"] >= thresh2

df["category"] = "neither"
df.loc[high1 & ~high2, "category"] = "pop1_specific"
df.loc[~high1 & high2, "category"] = "pop2_specific"
df.loc[high1 & high2,  "category"] = "both"

# Euclidean distance on z-score-normalized axes so both scans contribute equally
# regardless of their absolute selectivity ranges.
s1_z = (df["s1"] - df["s1"].mean()) / (df["s1"].std() + 1e-9)
s2_z = (df["s2"] - df["s2"].mean()) / (df["s2"].std() + 1e-9)
df["r"] = np.sqrt(s1_z**2 + s2_z**2)

# ── Select auto-labeled points ────────────────────────────────────────────────
def top_n_by(sub_df, sort_col, n):
    return sub_df.nlargest(n, sort_col)

auto_label_rows = pd.concat([
    top_n_by(df[df["category"] == "pop1_specific"], "s1", N_LABEL),
    top_n_by(df[df["category"] == "pop2_specific"], "s2", N_LABEL),
    top_n_by(df[df["category"] == "both"], "r", N_LABEL),  # rank by Euclidean distance
]).drop_duplicates(subset=["rec1", "rec2"])

# ── Global top-N pairs per axis (starred) ─────────────────────────────────────
top_s1_rows = df.nlargest(N_TOP_OVERALL, "s1")
top_s2_rows = df.nlargest(N_TOP_OVERALL, "s2")
top_overall_rows = pd.concat([top_s1_rows, top_s2_rows]).drop_duplicates(
    subset=["rec1", "rec2"]
)

# ── User-specified pairs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Look up each requested pair in df, checking both (A, B) and (B, A) orderings.
user_rows_list = []
for req_rec1, req_rec2 in HIGHLIGHT_PAIRS:
    match = df[
        ((df["rec1"] == req_rec1) & (df["rec2"] == req_rec2))
        | ((df["rec1"] == req_rec2) & (df["rec2"] == req_rec1))
    ]
    if match.empty:
        print(f"Warning: requested pair ({req_rec1}, {req_rec2}) not found in data.")
    else:
        user_rows_list.append(match.iloc[[0]])
user_rows = (
    pd.concat(user_rows_list).drop_duplicates(subset=["rec1", "rec2"])
    if user_rows_list
    else pd.DataFrame(columns=df.columns)
)

# ── Plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))

# Base scatter for "neither", pop1, pop2
for cat, style in {
    "neither":       dict(color="lightgray", zorder=1, alpha=0.6, s=18, label="Neither"),
    "pop1_specific": dict(color="#3A86FF",   zorder=3, alpha=0.85, s=40,
                          label=f"Selective for {POP_1_LABEL}"),
    "pop2_specific": dict(color="#FF9F1C",   zorder=3, alpha=0.85, s=40,
                          label=f"Selective for {POP_2_LABEL}"),
}.items():
    sub = df[df["category"] == cat]
    ax.scatter(sub["s1"], sub["s2"], **style)

# "both" category: size proportional to Euclidean distance
both_sub = df[df["category"] == "both"].copy()
if not both_sub.empty:
    r_min, r_max = both_sub["r"].min(), both_sub["r"].max()
    if r_max > r_min:
        sizes_both = 40 + 200 * (both_sub["r"] - r_min) / (r_max - r_min)
    else:
        sizes_both = np.full(len(both_sub), 80)
    ax.scatter(
        both_sub["s1"], both_sub["s2"],
        s=sizes_both, color="#E63946", zorder=4, alpha=0.95,
        label="Effective against both (size ∝ Euclidean dist.)",
    )
    # Reference arc at the Euclidean distance of the N_LABEL-th labeled "both" point
    labeled_both = both_sub.nlargest(N_LABEL, "r")
    if len(labeled_both) == N_LABEL:
        r_ref_z = labeled_both["r"].min()  # distance cutoff (z-score space)
        # Convert back: arc in data space using the normalized axis scales
        s1_std = df["s1"].std() + 1e-9
        s2_std = df["s2"].std() + 1e-9
        s1_mean = df["s1"].mean()
        s2_mean = df["s2"].mean()
        theta_arc = np.linspace(0, np.pi / 2, 200)
        arc_x = s1_mean + r_ref_z * s1_std * np.cos(theta_arc)
        arc_y = s2_mean + r_ref_z * s2_std * np.sin(theta_arc)
        # Clip arc to data bounds
        x_mask = (arc_x >= ax.get_xlim()[0]) & (arc_y >= ax.get_ylim()[0])
        ax.plot(
            arc_x[x_mask], arc_y[x_mask],
            color="#E63946", linewidth=1.2, linestyle=":", alpha=0.55,
            zorder=3, label=f"Both: top-{N_LABEL} Euclidean cutoff",
        )

# User-specified pairs (purple diamonds)
if not user_rows.empty:
    ax.scatter(
        user_rows["s1"], user_rows["s2"],
        marker="D", s=80, color="#9B5DE5", zorder=6, alpha=1.0,
        label="User-specified pairs",
    )

# Global top-N stars (gold)
ax.scatter(
    top_overall_rows["s1"], top_overall_rows["s2"],
    marker="*", s=220, color="#FFD700", edgecolors="#888", linewidths=0.5,
    zorder=7, alpha=1.0, label=f"Top-{N_TOP_OVERALL} per axis",
)

# Threshold lines
ax.axvline(thresh1, color="steelblue", linewidth=1.0, linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
ax.axhline(thresh2, color="darkorange", linewidth=1.0, linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

# ── Annotations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
all_label_rows = pd.concat([
    auto_label_rows,
    top_overall_rows,
    user_rows,
]).drop_duplicates(subset=["rec1", "rec2"])

for _, row in all_label_rows.iterrows():
    label = f"({row['rec1']}, {row['rec2']})"
    txt = ax.text(
        row["s1"], row["s2"], label,
        fontsize=7, ha="left", va="bottom",
    )
    txt.set_path_effects([pe.withStroke(linewidth=2, foreground="white")])

ax.set_xlabel(f"Selectivity vs. {POP_1_LABEL}", fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel(f"Selectivity vs. {POP_2_LABEL}", fontsize=13)
ax.set_title(
    f"Off-target Population Selectivity Comparison\nTarget: {TARG_CELL_TYPE}  "
    f"(threshold = {HIGH_PERCENTILE}th percentile)",
    fontsize=12, pad=12,
)
ax.tick_params(labelsize=11)
# ax.legend(fontsize=10, framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
# Tabular summary of all annotated receptor pairs
display_cols = ["rec1", "rec2", "s1", "s2", "r", "category"]
summary = all_label_rows[display_cols].copy()
summary.columns = [
    "Receptor 1", "Receptor 2", POP_1_LABEL, POP_2_LABEL,
    "Euclidean dist. (norm.)", "Category",
]
summary = summary.sort_values("Category").reset_index(drop=True)
summary[[POP_1_LABEL, POP_2_LABEL, "Euclidean dist. (norm.)"]] = summary[
    [POP_1_LABEL, POP_2_LABEL, "Euclidean dist. (norm.)"]
].round(4)
print(summary.to_string(index=False))
```
